arts.ttl: Ethan Nwaneri could miss 10 Arsenal matches as Mikel Arteta delivers injury update
arts.sum: Sports Mole looks at the Arsenal games Ethan Nwaneri could miss as Mikel Arteta confirms that the 17-year-old will be sidelined for a few weeks with a muscle injury.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta admits something rare just happened in Arsenal training he has not seen before
Alan Shearer in doubt over Ange Postecoglou's Newcastle claim
arts.sum: However, while Newcastle goalscoring legend Alan Shearer admits he's not a fan of the rule, he felt Postecoglou was wrong to bemoan the decision - and pointed out that he failed to mention what Shearer felt was a stonewall penalty for United which was not given. The presumption was that he was furious with big refereeing decisions, with the ball clearly hitting Joelinton's hand before Anthony Gordon's equaliser.

arts.ttl: Arteta: Arsenal 'alert' in market as injuries mount
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has said that Arsenal are "very alert" in the January transfer market after the London club suffered a fresh injury blow with Ethan Nwaneri out for a "few weeks."

arts.ttl: How Arne Slot sides typically perform in second half of the season as Arsenal sit six points behind Liverpool
Nwaneri out for a few weeks | 'Arsenal in a position to act in window'
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has confirmed Arsenal midfielder Ethan Nwaneri will be sidelined for a few weeks with a muscular injury.

arts.ttl: Newcastle’s Carabao Cup chance comes at crucial moment in club’s history
arts.sum: Eddie Howe desperate to end club’s long trophy drought as decision awaits over potential rebuild of St James’ Park By the time Sunday 16 March dawns, Newcastle United’s short-, medium- and long-term future should be considerably clearer. The day itself will see Eddie Howe either leading his team out at Wembley in the Carabao Cup final or rewatching videos of the previous day’s scheduled Premier League fixture at home to Crystal Palace. It all hinges on a two-part Carabao Cup semi-final against Arsenal, with the first leg in north London on Tuesday night. Howe has frequently reiterated his “burning desire” to end a club trophy drought stretching back to 1969 and the European Fairs Trophy. “Arsenal’s a massive game for us,” he said on Monday. “We’re under no illusions as to how big this is for our season.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta suffers fresh blow as Arsenal learn extent of Ethan Nwaneri injury
arts.sum: Ethan Nwaneri scored in Arsenal's 1-1 draw with Brighton on Saturday, having been given increased minutes since the Gunners lost Bukayo Saka to injury until the spring

arts.ttl: Newcastle United supremo's transfer statement sparks candid training ground admission
arts.sum: Eddie Howe sat down today and faced the media for the third time already in 2025 and he could be forgiven at groaning for having to face another series of questions about recruitment in the now active January transfer window. Howe had already warned us that Newcastle won't be able to spend big and on the back of last January when nobody came through the door, and last summer when only free transfers like John Ruddy and Lloyd Kelly arrived plus a swap deal for Odysseas Vlachodimos along with £15million purchase of Will Osula, few will need too many reminders on the narrative.

arts.ttl: Newcastle chiefs see transfer need as Howe faced with emotional exit truth
arts.sum: Martin Dubravka is likely to leave in the coming days - despite being the club's current No 1 - and bigger exits will be required to pave the way for outfield spending in the short term. In recent days Newcastle United's minority owners and boardroom chiefs have sat in the plush directors' boxes of Old Trafford and the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and watched the Magpies beat two big, big rivals.
